name: Genesis Information System via the Resistance Isomorphism
description: An independent research programme investigating whether number theory emerges as the erasure-shadow of a quantity-free foundational information system (the Genesis Information System), posited behind an epistemic barrier that is unfalsifiable by construction. The central bridge is the Resistance Isomorphism, Omega(1/n) = ln n, read as the Landauer erasure cost of an integer. The programme develops the formal consequences of the posit under a strict explore-then-criticise discipline, grading every claim as Fact, Reading, or Lead. Not peer-reviewed; all Python experiments are reproducible. No claim is made on the Riemann Hypothesis.

author.roleName: Originating theory, axioms, and directional intuitions

citation: Pisani, Alexander, with Claude (Anthropic). Genesis Information System via the Resistance Isomorphism. 2026. CC BY 4.0.
